source.definition: A polite expression used when parting, originally meaning 'God be with you' but now a general farewell.

explanation.sourceText: The word 'goodbye' originated as a contraction of the phrase 'God be with ye,' used as a farewell blessing in English.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „goodbye” provine din contracția expresiei „God be with ye”, folosită ca o binecuvântare la despărțire în limba engleză.

target.definition: Eine höfliche Verabschiedung, die 'bis zum Wiedersehen' bedeutet.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian phrase 'La revedere' derives from the Latin verb 'revidēre', composed of the prefix 're-' (again) and 'vidēre' (to see), literally meaning 'until we see again'.
explanation.targetText: Expresia românească „La revedere” provine din verbul latin 'revidēre', format din prefixul 're-' (din nou) și 'vidēre' (a vedea), însemnând literal 'până ne vedem din nou'.

examples.sourceText: I always say 'Goodbye' when I leave.
examples.targetText: Eu spun întotdeauna 'La revedere' când plec.

examples.sourceText: Goodbye and have a nice day!
examples.targetText: La revedere și o zi frumoasă în continuare!

learningTips.text: Remember 'La revedere' sounds like 're-vedere' which means 'see again'—just like 'goodbye' originally meant 'God be with you' as a parting wish.
The Romanian word 'vedere' means 'to see', similar to the English word 'video' which also relates to seeing.
The prefix 're-' in Romanian means 'again', just like in English words such as 'redo' or 'review', helping you understand 'La revedere' as 'until we see again'.
Think of saying 'La revedere' as an invitation to meet again, similar to how 'goodbye' was originally a blessing wishing someone well until next time.
